lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings outerlander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This place? Why, this is Rellekka! Homeland of all Fremennik! I do not recognise your face outerlander; Where do you come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not important...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm... I will not press the issue then outerlander. How may my tribe and I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Quests, you say outerlander? Well, I would not call it a quest as such, but if you are brave of heart and strong of body, perhaps...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No, you would not be interested. Forget I said anything, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I would be very interested to hear what you have to offer.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You would? These are unusual sentiments to hear from an outerlander! My suggestion was going to be that if you crave adventure and battle,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: and your heart sings for glory, then perhaps you would be interested in joining our clan, and becoming a Fremennik yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would that involve exactly?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there are two ways to become a member of our clan and call yourself a Fremennik: be born a Fremennik, or be voted in by our council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I've missed the first way, but how can I get the council of elders to vote to let me join your clan?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, that I cannot answer myself. You will need to speak to each of them and see what they require of you as proof of your dedication.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There are twelve council members around this village; you will need to gain a majority vote of at least seven councillors in your favour.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So what say you? Give me the word, and I will tell all of my tribe of your intentions, be they yea or nay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I would enjoy the challenge of becoming an honorary Fremennik. Where and how do I start?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As I say outerlander, you must find and speak to the twelve members of the council of elders, and see what tasks they might set you.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you can gain the support of seven of the twelve, then you will be accepted as one of us without question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way do I want to become like YOU! Forget you, and forget your whole stupid town! You're a bunch of stupid primitives, from what I've seen!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I am sorry to say that I expected such a response from an outerlander. I thought perhaps you might have been different to all the others...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right. I really couldn't care less.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It is as I thought outerlander. Your kind care nothing for the Fremennik, and we feel the same about you. This is the way of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why will no-one talk to me?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not take it personally, outerlander! We are a simple people, and it is our experience that keeping ourselves to ourselves is best. This is why speaking to outerlanders is forbidden.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not wish to enter war with the outerlanders and their strange magics, so we limit all unauthorised communication.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how come you're talking to me?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, this is because I am the chieftain. I am the one who authorises contact. You will not find many of my tribe so forthcoming with you, as I.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there a way for you to authorise your tribe to talk to me then?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there is one way... but I doubt it is of any interest to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I would be very interested to hear what you have to offer.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You would? These are unusual sentiments to hear from an outerlander! My suggestion was going to be that if you crave adventure and battle,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: and your heart sings for glory, then perhaps you would be interested in joining our clan, and becoming a Fremennik yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would that involve exactly?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there are two ways to become a member of our clan and call yourself a Fremennik: be born a Fremennik, or be voted in by our council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I've missed the first way, but how can I get the council of elders to vote to let me join your clan?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, that I cannot answer myself. You will need to speak to each of them and see what they require of you as proof of your dedication.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There are twelve council members around this village; you will need to gain a majority vote of at least seven councillors in your favour.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So what say you? Give me the word, and I will tell all of my tribe of your intentions, be they yea or nay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I would enjoy the challenge of becoming an honorary Fremennik. Where and how do I start?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As I say outerlander, you must find and speak to the twelve members of the council of elders, and see what tasks they might set you.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you can gain the support of seven of the twelve, then you will be accepted as one of us without question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way do I want to become like YOU! Forget you, and forget your whole stupid town! You're a bunch of stupid primitives, from what I've seen!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I am sorry to say that I expected such a response from an outerlander. I thought perhaps you might have been different to all the others...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you call me outerlander?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not take it as an insult, outerlander. For us, this town is the center of all. Anyone not born here, or that we do not know and trust is an outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it basically means stranger?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Aye, but that is an outerlander word. We do not judge someones ways by their name. It simply means one from the outerlands, or one not from Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I know our customs are foreign to you; perhaps you would like me to explain something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek an army to destroy Asgarnia!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We will have no part in the squabbles of outerlander tribes. Their magics are strange and deadly to us, as we learnt long ago.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If this is all that you came here for, then you have had a wasted journey. If you are looking for something else, then speak now for my patience dwindles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the Fremennik?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It is the name we call ourselves. Outerlanders tend to call us 'Barbarian', but they do not understand our ways...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice hat!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not mock me outerlander; this helm has saved my life more times than I care to recall right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: As a matter of fact [Fremennik name], I do have a quest for you. Are you interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I’m ready for adventure

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: Excellent, now-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will it be this time? Joining warring islands?

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: I’m a fan of you enthusiasm, [Fremennik name], but-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you have me slay a mighty daggermouth? Or do you need my expertise in conquering untamed lands?

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: Nothing quite that fancy I’m afraid, [Fremennik name]. I just need you to investigate a minor disturbance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: Freygerd and Reeso run the sandpit a little north of here. They mentioned they heard some rustling by their hut lat night and wanted an official to investigate. You’ve proven yourself more than worthy of our people’s trust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn’t sound so bad. I’ll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: Thank you, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Brundt
lines.text: Very well. Return when you wish to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there Brundt the Chieftain. Could you help me out with a Seal of Passage?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: A Seal of Passage? Whatever for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've heard that there have been great troubles between your people and that of the Moon Clan. I was hoping to help. And so I need the Seal of Passage to show my intentions.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah [Fremennik name], many have tried, but The Moon Clan are by no means a sharing group of people. They do everything by magic, something we never use. But they are so secretive about it. If only they would share, we

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: wouldn't have to kill them all the time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have that seal then?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you are to bring peace to our two warring clans, then perhaps it is best that you do...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost my Seal of Passage!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Then you had better take a replacement and be less careless in future.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well if it isn't [Fremennik name]. I've heard of your exploits in the Lunar Isles!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Already? News really does travel fast!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Thank you so very much, I think we can really make progress now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No worries. I am pleased I could help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, erm, could you help me out with a Seal of Passage?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Seal of Passage, well I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ok, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did the meeting go?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It went surprisingly well! We are at least at the point where we will freely acknowledge each other's existence now.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You are definitely a great asset to the Fremennik, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost V's Shield.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I don't think you have [Fremennik name]. I suggest you try looking for it again.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You really should be more careful [Fremennik name]. Luckily for you I found it again but you'll have to pay to get it back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: 50,000 coins. Consider it a lesson in looking after things better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Here you go [Fremennik name]. Use it well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit too much I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hello again [brother/sister] [Fremennik name]! I hope your travels have brought you wealth and joy! What compels you to visit me on this day?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just came to say hello.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well met, [brother/sister] [Fremennik name]. It is always good to see your face in glorious Rellekka once more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Not at the moment [Fremennik name]. Rest assured, should your services to the Fremennik be required, I will call upon you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice hat!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Aye, that it is [brother/sister] [Fremennik name]. Should you desire one of your own, you should go to Skulgrimen's shop and see what they have available!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I hear the history of your people?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Why, of course [Fremennik name]! Do not say 'your people' like that, for you are now a Fremennik yourself! What did you want to hear of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm interested in finding out about that mysterious warrior I fought as part of Thorvalds' combat trial. His name is Koschei I believe.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah... the deathless one... We do not even know if Koschei is his name, when we found him he had no memory, he was simply repeating that single word.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Whatever happened to him must have rattled his soul so hard that his memory ran away from him, and he has not yet found it's hiding place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how exactly did you find him?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, it was a raiding party like any other. We had just struck one of the smaller northern islands to harvest supplies by combat, as our laws allow,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: when we saw a figure in the icey waters. As we were at least a 3 week sail from any port, and saw no other ships nearby, he must have been there some time.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We could not leave someone to the cold death of the ocean, even an outerlander, so we fished him aboard. He muttered continually, but his words made no sense.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: He had no clothes or possessions with him, and seemed to have been burnt somehow before he had landed in the water.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I cannot help but wonder what burnt him, for as you may have seen, he is incredibly powerful and resistant to damage by any means I know of!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Anyway, we decided to bring him back to Rellekka, for he did not seem of good health or sound mind, and simply repeated the word Koschei over and over.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yrsa tended to him for many weeks, until one day it was as though a new soul had entered his burnt and broken body.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: He suddenly left her house one day, fully healed, and able to speak to us as though nothing had happened to him.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: He still had no recollection of how he had come to be in the icey ocean, or of who he was, or where he had come from, but this did not matter to us or him.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: He took his trials of adulthood, as you did, and has been a boon to our clan ever since. Someday I fear he may regain his memory, and leave us for his past...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: But I will always respect any decision he decides to make should that day ever come upon us. Until then, he is more than welcome to stay here.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So, [Fremennik name]. Was there anything more you wished to hear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like to hear of the lands across the ocean to the North.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, I think the best thing for you would be to go to the docks and ask to join one of our longboats on a journey.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: My affairs are concentrated mostly on Rellekka, so my knowledge is not great, but I will happily share what I do know with you [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: North of this town the atmosphere becomes bitterly cold. There are a number of small inhabited islands that we have sea routes with.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The largest of these is possibly Miscellania, although I hear that there has been some recent incident with their monarch, but I don't know the details.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There is also of course the island of the moon clan, who change with the tides. They are our bitterest enemies, and we are currently at war with them.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: They are an evil people, equally cursed and blessed by the magic in their blood. Beyond those islands, there is the large frozen wasteland we call Acheron.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It is an inhospitable land, and you will need an agile and sturdy body just to keep alive in it's perils.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As I say, my knowledge outside of this town is rather limited, and I know no more than this. Was there anything else you wished to hear tell of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like to hear a little of the history between the Fremenniks and the outerlanders.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, now that is something I know a great deal about. Believe it or not, all outerlanders were once orginally Fremenniks.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: When first man came to these lands all were Fremenniks, and followed our ways. We lived a happy life, and never settled in one place for long.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We travelled by boat along the coastlines, never taking more from the land than could be regrown by the same time in the following year.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: However, many moons past, some of our tribesmen were weary of constantly travelling the lands, and decided to build themselves permanent homes.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Unfortunately, the other races that had also migrated here did not like this. They waged war against us at every opportunity.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We were driven mostly to the coastlines, where we could escape by boat when attacked. Remember, at this time we had no real way to defend against constant attack.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Then one day one of our Seers, whose name has been lost in the telling of this tale, discovered a cave full of strange rocks.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This rock would never be consumed, no matter how much we took, and sparkled at its peak, and broke off in small and regular sized chunks when mined.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Stranger than this, we found that these rocks would become filled with power when taken to certain places across this land!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There was a terrible argument in our tribe! Some said that we should take as many of these rocks as we could, and use them in defending against our enemies attacks!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Others said that we had found a place that belonged only to the gods, and that should we steal what was not ours we would find only torment and misery.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We voted on this, and decided that it was in the best interests of the tribe to leave these strange rocks where they lay, for the gods can be spiteful and cruel,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: especially to those who do not treat them with the respect they desire. Some of our number refused to accept this ruling by our council however.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: They began to mine these rocks, and set up transport systems to the various places of power that enchanted them.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: They even created temples at each of these places! This was going too far! We had no choice, but to expel them from our tribe forever!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We turned our backs upon them, and let them know they would never be welcome back to our tribes until they had released themselves from the rocks.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This is the tale of how the outerlanders came about; through stealing from the gods, and from betraying our ideals. This is why we show them no trust.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Was there something else you wished to hear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, history isn't really my thing.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well let me know should you wish to hear our lore and histories. We value them highly.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Sorry, but your trail is not over quite yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Find the answer to this question for me.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Please enter the answer to the question.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Wrong answer!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Perfect!
